name: Shotstack Video Editing API
what_it_does: Programmatic video editing API — enabling developers to create, edit, and render videos at scale using a JSON timeline model, with text overlays, transitions, merged data templates, text-to-speech, AI-powered scene generation, and multi-track timeline editing, designed for automated bulk video production (personalized videos, social media clips, highlight reels).
use_cases: Generating personalized videos at scale via Shotstack API for marketing automation campaigns
Creating social media clip videos from templates via Shotstack API for content automation
Producing property listing videos from photos via Shotstack API for real estate platforms
Rendering sports highlight reels automatically via Shotstack API for media workflows
Building video report generators via Shotstack API for data visualization delivery
not_for: Live video streaming (Shotstack renders pre-built videos — not live)
Complex professional editing requiring frame-accurate control beyond JSON API
Teams needing final-cut-quality rendering (Shotstack targets programmatic/automated video)
best_when: A developer or platform needs to automate bulk video production — generating hundreds or thousands of personalized, template-based videos programmatically from data, without manual editing.
avoid_when: You need live streaming, frame-accurate professional editing, or complex multi-layer compositing beyond what JSON timelines support.

auth.notes: API key from Shotstack account. x-api-key header auth. Separate sandbox and production API keys. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes. Webhook on render completion with configurable URL. SDKs for JavaScript, PHP, Python, Ruby, Java. Sandbox environment for testing. Timeline editor (GUI) for template creation. Merge fields for data-driven video generation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier: sandbox environment with watermarked output (unlimited)
pricing.paid_tiers: Basic from $49/month (300 renders); Pro $149/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Australian company (Sydney). Founded 2019 by Andreas Liff, Mark Eggers, and Mark Bailey. Raised $2M seed. AI-powered video generation. JSON timeline model for programmatic video editing. Multi-track (video, audio, overlay tracks). Merge fields for bulk personalization. Text-to-speech built-in. AI scene generation (text-to-video clips). Asset library (stock footage, music). Used by real estate platforms, sports media, marketing automation tools. SDK for 5 languages. Sandbox watermarked output for free testing.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with JSON timeline model. x-api-key header auth. Webhook on completion. SDKs for 5 languages. Sandbox for testing (watermarked). Good documentation with example timelines. Async render model — poll or webhook. Concurrent render limits by plan. Australian company.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Render status reads idempotent by render ID. Render creation not idempotent — each submit creates new render. Webhook delivery may retry. Asset reads idempotent by ID.
known_agent_gotchas: Async render model — submit then poll or use webhook; no synchronous response
Separate sandbox (x-api-key sandbox key) and production keys — use sandbox for testing
Render time depends on video duration and complexity — can take minutes
Concurrent render limits by plan — queue renders carefully for bulk generation
Australian data residency may not meet EU data residency requirements

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. x-api-key header auth. Separate sandbox and production keys — good isolation. No OAuth2 or scope granularity. Video content processed through Shotstack infrastructure — evaluate for sensitive content. Webhook URLs should use HTTPS. Australian company — evaluate for EU data residency requirements. No compliance certifications documented.
